title: Smith theory does NOT obstruct: Q-acyclic ≠ Z/2-acyclic; clarify the false NO-argument

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: The prior strategy memory (docs_overview.md, docs_strategies.md, and all 9 failed attempts in docs_timeline.md) tries to derive a contradiction via Smith theory: the order-2 deck transformation acts freely on $\widetilde M$, but Smith says a free $\mathbb{Z}/2$ action on a $\mathbb{Z}/2$-acyclic finite-dimensional space has a fixed point. **This is the wrong direction.** The hypothesis is only $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclicity, and $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic does NOT imply $\mathbb{Z}/2$-acyclic (the cover may carry nontrivial mod-2 homology). A solution must explicitly establish: (i) Smith theory is inapplicable here; (ii) similarly the compact Lefschetz fixed-point theorem (which would give $L(\gamma)=1\neq0$ from $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclicity) does NOT apply because $\widetilde M$ is noncompact ($\Gamma$ infinite) and $\gamma$ is a proper cocompact deck map. Provide a concrete finite-dimensional $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic complex with a free $\mathbb{Z}/2$ action (necessarily with nonzero mod-2 homology) to demonstrate consistency. This reorients the whole problem toward the affirmative answer.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved.** I added a dedicated subsection "Why the classical fixed-point obstructions do not apply (issue q7-3)" right after the naive-formulation correction, addressing all three requested points explicitly and rigorously.

**(i) Smith theory is inapplicable (paragraph O1).** The deck action of an involution gamma is free on tilde M, so Smith gives only that tilde M is NOT Z/2-acyclic. This says nothing about Q-acyclicity because Q-acyclic does NOT imply Z/2-acyclic: over Q the averaging idempotent (1/2)(1+gamma^*) trivializes the relevant Tate cohomology, so there is no Smith theorem with Q coefficients. The mod-2 homology Smith forces is exactly that carried by the free local models W_F.

**(ii) The Lefschetz fixed-point theorem is inapplicable (paragraph O2).** I wrote out the tempting (false) argument L(gamma)=tr(gamma^* | H_0)=1 != 0 and then flagged precisely why it fails: tilde M is NON-COMPACT (Gamma is an infinite uniform lattice, tilde M / Gamma = M compact => tilde M noncompact), and gamma is a proper cocompact fixed-point-free deck map. Lefschetz-Hopf requires a compact ENR/manifold; on a noncompact space the alternating Q-trace is not a fixed-point index. The correct proper-cocompact invariant is the L^2/Gamma-Lefschetz number chi^{(2)}(M), which imposes no fixed point for a free action.

**(iii) Concrete consistency witness (paragraph C).**
- (C1) Compact quotient-level: V = presentation 2-complex of Z/2 = <a|a^2>, cellular chain Z--2-->Z--0-->Z, so H_*(V;Z) = (Z, Z/2, 0) => V is Q-acyclic with pi_1 = Z/2, chi=1; its universal cover W = S^2 (antipodal free Z/2) has H_*(S^2;Z/2) != 0 and chi=2. This is the simplest instance of the (W_F, V_F) local model.
- (C2) Cover-level: by the Euler-characteristic Lemma no COMPACT free Z/2 complex is itself Q-acyclic (chi would be 1 and even), so a self-Q-acyclic free Z/2 space must be noncompact — exactly the regime of tilde M. The construction of Thm (affirmative answer) yields precisely such a tilde M: finite-dimensional, noncompact, Q-acyclic, free Gamma-action, nonzero mod-2 homology. No fixed-point theorem is contradicted.

All numbers verified by direct chain-complex computation (presentation complex of Z/2, S^2 mod-2/rational homology, RP^n homology). Brace/environment balance checked; all internal \\ref labels resolve. (No LaTeX toolchain installed in workspace, so compilation was skipped, but the file is structurally balanced: 39 begin/39 end, 698/698 braces.)

This reorients the document to the affirmative direction as requested, by explicitly demolishing both false NO-arguments and supplying the consistency example.
